datasetCoverage: Official 14-county Upper Cumberland footprint plus central-area OpenStreetMap amenity counts around Cookeville (30 km radius) and county presidential-return weighting from 2000-2024.
highwayCorridors: SR-53 / US-25 approach to Cordell Hull
transitServices: Pick Up Upper Cumberland after-hours rideshare linkage
Connect Upper Cumberland intercity bus on I-40 / I-24 routes

politicalModel.source: County presidential results 2000-2024 from the MIT Election Lab rebuild published by JHK Forecasts.
politicalModel.interpretation: Weighted Democrat share seeds BLUFOR support, weighted Republican share seeds OPFOR support, and weighted independent share can lean slightly toward the minority at low propaganda or swing hard toward the majority when propaganda is high.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Hybrid midsize sedan reference for regional staff movement and courier runs.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Minimal crash structure and no ballistic protection; useful for low-signature liaison movement only.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Fuel and maintenance aligned to AAA 2024 cost bands; breakdown rate is a sim calibration estimate.
immersionFactors: Low fuel burden makes it attractive for courier missions.
Very poor survivability in contested movement.
Breakdowns are uncommon but mission-ending without support.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: AAA Your Driving Costs 2024 plus NHTSA passenger vehicle fatality pattern summary.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Compact crossover/SUV commonly used by county services and contractors.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Better crash geometry than sedans but still no meaningful protection from small arms or fragmentation.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Maintenance anchored to AAA compact SUV band; breakdown and survivability use public crash trends plus sim normalization.
immersionFactors: Common civilian utility platform for local government and relief groups.
Moderate cargo space helps with ad hoc resupply or casualty shuttle tasks.
Still highly vulnerable once roads are restricted.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: AAA Your Driving Costs 2024 and NHTSA passenger vehicle fatality comparison.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Crew-cab 4x4 half-ton pickup used by utilities, farms, and local contractors.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Useful payload and rough-road access but no armor; bed cargo is exposed to weather and theft.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Maintenance from AAA pickup band; other factors are representative fleet modeling estimates.
immersionFactors: Strong fit for volunteer, contractor, and utility support behavior.
Payload flexibility matters more than fuel efficiency.
Often present on farm-to-market and county roads.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: AAA Your Driving Costs 2024 with representative U.S. pickup operating assumptions.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Full-size diesel Type C/D school bus for evacuation or shuttle use.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: High crash survivability for occupants in civil traffic but effectively unprotected against deliberate attack.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Fuel economy and maintenance are fleet-style estimates intended for immersion, not audited Tennessee bus costs.
immersionFactors: Excellent for mass movement but hard to conceal.
Creates major route blockage when disabled.
Requires wider turning radii and stronger driver skill on back roads.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: Representative U.S. school bus fleet operations with public crash survivability patterns.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Regional Class 8 tractor-trailer used for fuel, food, and palletized freight movement.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Large mass helps in ordinary crashes but cab remains vulnerable and high-value cargo attracts interference.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Maintenance cost anchored to AAA commercial truck band; breakdown rate is a fleet-style simulation estimate.
immersionFactors: Best civilian bulk mover in the region.
Lane width, grades, and bridge approach geometry matter.
Breakdowns cause long clearance delays and immediate congestion spikes.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: AAA Your Driving Costs 2024 commercial trucking cost band plus representative long-haul operating assumptions.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Oshkosh Joint Light Tactical Vehicle general-purpose variant.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: High survivability against blast and small-arms threats relative to civilian light vehicles.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Capability profile follows public Oshkosh JLTV material; maintenance and reliability values are sim calibration estimates.
immersionFactors: Strong protection but higher fuel demand than civilian utility vehicles.
Road speed remains constrained by convoy discipline, not raw top speed.
Good fit for escort, liaison, and contested courier tasks.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: Official Oshkosh Defense JLTV brochure plus simulation-only maintenance and reliability calibration.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: Family of Medium Tactical Vehicles 5-ton 6x6 cargo configuration.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Good tactical mobility and moderate crew protection depending on kit, but not a substitute for armored escort.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Range and speed follow public FMTV specifications; cost and breakdown values are simulation calibration estimates.
immersionFactors: Backbone truck for configured loads and routine distribution.
Payload efficiency is good but long climbs and muddy approaches hurt pace.
Breakdowns accumulate when maintenance discipline slips.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: Public FMTV spec references with estimated maintenance and reliability tuning.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: HEMTT A4 tanker/fueler family for bulk Class III distribution.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Moderate crew survivability with very high mission criticality and severe secondary effects if disabled.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Range follows public HEMTT family references; other values are immersion-focused estimates for heavy tactical logistics.
immersionFactors: Losses or delays ripple into every other fuel-dependent platform.
Large signature and tanker role increase targeting pressure.
Recovery requirements are heavy when it goes NMC.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: Public HEMTT family literature plus simulation-only sustainment calibration.

transportProfiles.referenceVehicle: M1A2 Abrams main battle tank as a high-demand protected mover.
transportProfiles.protectionSummary: Exceptional crew protection in direct combat but extremely demanding fuel and maintenance burden.
transportProfiles.estimateBasis: Fuel burn follows the public Army Abrams fuel-use guide; maintenance and reliability rates are simulation tuning estimates.
immersionFactors: Fuel burden dominates route planning.
Idle time matters almost as much as movement for sustainment planning.
Recovery, transport, and maintenance capacity become campaign-level constraints.
transportProfiles.sourceSummary: U.S. Army Abrams fuel-burn guide with simulation-estimated maintenance and breakdown rates.

featureHighlights.name: Upper Cumberland Regional Airport (SRB)
featureHighlights.notes: Regional airport near the Putnam-White county line with a 6,000-foot asphalt runway.

featureHighlights.name: Crossville Memorial Airport / Whitson Field (CSV)
featureHighlights.notes: General aviation and corporate airport in Crossville.

featureHighlights.name: Caney Fork & Western Railroad
featureHighlights.notes: Regional rail service continuing the former Sparta Branch corridor.

featureHighlights.name: Connect Upper Cumberland
featureHighlights.notes: Intercity bus service linking the region westbound on I-40 and I-24 corridors.

featureHighlights.name: Defeated Creek Park
featureHighlights.notes: USACE Cordell Hull Lake campground with 155 campsites.

featureHighlights.name: Tailwater Recreation Area
featureHighlights.notes: USACE day-use recreation area below Cordell Hull Dam near Carthage.
